{"query":"France social media ban under 15 teenagers legislation timeline Macron fast-track 2026 status","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1829,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.france24.com/en/tv-shows/tech-24/20260125-france-set-to-fast-track-under-15s-social-media-ban","title":"France set to fast-track under-15s social media ban - Tech 24 - France 24","content":"France set to fast-track under-15s social media ban
"Our teenagers' emotions are not to be manipulated – either by American platforms or by Chinese algorithms."
Meta said it removed nearly 550,000 under-16 accounts in one month; that's around 330,000 on Instagram, 173,500 on Facebook and nearly 40,000 on Threads."},{"url":"https://www.cbsnews.com/news/france-macron-ban-social-media-kids-under-15/","title":"France's Macron pushes for fast-track ban on social media for kids under 15 - CBS News","content":"Jan 25, 2026 — France's Macron pushes for fast-track ban on social media for kids under 15 January 25, 2026 … age of 15 can enter into force in September at ...
In a video released late Saturday by French broadcaster BFM-TV, Macron said he had asked his government to initiate an accelerated procedure so that the proposed legislation can move as quickly as possible and be passed by the Senate in time.
"We are banning social media for under-15s, and we are going to ban mobile phones in our high schools," Macron said.
Australia recently implemented the world's first social media ban for teens younger than 16, with its government urging Meta, Snapchat, TikTok, X and Youtube to take reasonable steps to exclude Australian account holders from their platforms if they don't meet the age requirement."},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/france-aims-ban-under-15s-social-media-september-2026-le-monde-reports-2025-12-31/","title":"Macron wants to ban under-15s from social media from September 2026, Le Monde reports | Reuters","content":"PARIS, Dec 31 (Reuters) - France plans to ban children under 15 from social media sites and to prohibit mobile phones in high schools from September 2026, local media reported on Wednesday, moves that underscore rising public angst over the impact of online harms on minors."},{"url":"https://www.courthousenews.com/french-lawmakers-pass-bill-banning-social-media-for-under-15s/","title":"French lawmakers pass bill banning social media for under-15s | Courthouse News Service","content":"French lawmakers pass bill banning social media for under-15s
Authorities want the measures to be enforced from the start of the 2026 school year in September.
The legislation, which also provides for a ban on mobile phones in high schools, would make France the second country to take such a step following Australia’s ban for under-16s in December.
Former Prime Minister Gabriel Attal, who leads Macron’s Renaissance party in the lower house, said he hoped the Senate would pass the bill by mid-February so that the ban could come into force on Sept.
Former Prime Minister Elisabeth Borne expressed reservations about the measure on Monday."},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/pixels/article/2026/03/31/france-eyes-ban-on-social-media-for-under-15s_6751995_13.html","title":"France eyes social media ban for under-15s","content":"President Emmanuel Macron is hoping France will become one of the first European Union countries to ban social media for under-15s, following similar moves in Australia and Indonesia.
But even if the legislation is adopted, questions remain over whether it could be enforced.
The lower house, the Assemblée Nationale, passed legislation in January demanding that all social media platforms refuse new users under 15 and suspend accounts belonging to children under that age.
Hard-left lawmaker Arnaud Saint-Martin said, at the time, that such a ban would amount to "digital paternalism" and be an "overly simplistic" response to the negative impacts of technology.
While backing France's right to impose such a ban, the European Commission in January said that any enforcement would lie with the EU, provided the bill conforms to the bloc's laws.
In December, Australia became the first country in the world to require TikTok, YouTube, Snapchat and other top sites to remove accounts held by under-16s, or face heavy fines."},{"url":"https://www.aa.com.tr/en/europe/macron-moves-to-fast-track-bill-banning-social-media-for-under-15s-in-france/3809848","title":"Macron moves to fast-track bill banning social media for ...","content":"Jan 24, 2026 — 24 January 2026•Update: 24 January 2026. World   Asia-Pacific   Middle East   Europe   Americas   Africa. မြန်မာဘာသာ. # France passes social media ban for under-15s but will it become law? Europe  19:55, 01-Apr-2026. French senators have approved a draft law that will ban social media access for children under the age of 15, joining countries around the world in considering a move unpopular with many teenagers but supported by some parents and teachers. It will see France follow Australia, whose world-first ban for under-16s on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into force in December. The lower house National Assembly in January passed legislation demanding all social media platforms refuse new users under 15 and suspend accounts belonging to children under that age. Even if the French law passes, putting it into place will be tricky. In December, Australia became the first country in the world to require TikTok, YouTube, Snapchat and other top sites to remove accounts held by under-16s, or face heavy fines.","score":0.8124067,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/pixels/article/2026/01/24/france-s-proposed-social-media-ban-for-under-15s-to-be-accelerated_6749764_13.html","title":"France's proposed social media ban for under-15s to be ...","content":"Jan 24, 2026 — President Emmanuel Macron said the government would use an accelerated procedure with the goal of enacting the ban in September.","score":0.7997713,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.65,"request_id":"28e22433-1124-412d-8be7-a3b85a365c4d"}
